techmeme-2022.text: a16z's Marc Andreessen and Packy McCormick failing to coherently explain Web3 use cases demonstrates the hollowness of the space, often obscured by boosterism
You Can Give People What They Want. Or You Can Give Them Web3

I wrote big ole newsletter about “the hollow abstraction” of Web3 and those videos of its biggest boosters struggling to articulate a concrete vision for their next big thing https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...

@ModeledBehavior I'm just so tired of the unfalsifiable crypto defenses that try to spin early failure as a signal of later success I didn't make my varsity basketball team. Neither did Michael Jordan. Can you imagine if I claimed my shittiness at bball was PROOF I would BECOME Jordan?!?

Today, as it stands, web3 has real use cases. Jumping back into the web3 use case debate with the first of a two-parter. Part I is all about disruption theory and the use cases that exist today. https://www.notboring.co/... https://twitter.com/...

“Finally I realized there's a pattern here: I keep making things that people don't want” Interesting piece from ⁦@cwarzel⁩ about a major existential problem in the web3 universe: Many of its most powerful boosters struggle to justify its purpose https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...

This is a fine point, but it means last few years have represented a massive mistake from retail crypto investors who failed to digest this. “This takes a really long time” is asymmetrically emphasized advice, for some reason we hear it more on the way down than the way up. https://twitter.com/...

Another point about “crypto's early failures are just like the Internet's early failures...” The Internet also had MASSIVE early fundamental successes—Amazon, Yahoo, AOL, eBay, et al, grew REVENUE phenomenally fast, not just market caps. Where are the crypto fundamentals? https://twitter.com/...

1 thing I tried to get at is just how information dense the crypto booster world is. Reading through whitepapers & posts and long tweet threads i feel like i'm being ddos'd by marketing language. And all of it does a good job obscuring what's really there https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...
that's why i think i enjoy those viral clips of investors struggling to articulate a web 3 vision. the interviewers give them no place for their ideas to hide. https://twitter.com/...

“What makes the videos so cathartic is that the interviewers give McCormick and Andreessen no place for their arguments to hide. The interviewers are asking very simple, rational questions, and the interviewees are unable to answer them without abstract language” Amazing read 👇 https://twitter.com/...

“He sees the crypto world as emblematic of a fundamental problem in startups, where founders and venture capitalists become so enamored with a vague initial idea that they never stop to consider its utility.” https://twitter.com/...
i thought this bit from @liron about founders who basically leapfrog the idea phase was good to help understand some of the mentality here (beyond just greed) https://twitter.com/...

“I cannot stop watching videos of Web3 boosters failing to explain the usefulness of the technology. I realize this is petty, but the videos are deeply cathartic,” @cwarzel writes in the latest issue of Galaxy Brain. https://on.theatln.tc/tWDttpD
Web3 boosterism resembles the underlying ponzinomics of crypto itself—always requiring new “use-cases” to obscure the hollowness of the whole enterprise. https://twitter.com/...
This is excellent from @cwarzel https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ... and also a helpful preview of (part of) this week's Recode Media pod.
“What makes the videos so cathartic for a crypto skeptic is that the interviewers give McCormick and Andreessen no place for their arguments to hide.” https://twitter.com/...
That's because there's no “there” there. The “Hollow Abstraction” of Web3 https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...
Amazing how similar the hype-talk about Web3 and 5G is. Lots of promises, yet no convincing argument or proof why it's better. Just word salad. https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...

Today's installment of the great Web3 use case debate: Sequoia crypto partner @shaunmmaguire gives his pitch to @zachweinberg and @loganbartlett on @cartoonavatars Some highlights: * Casual money laundering * Says Web3 use case is indie game * Implies @a16z's $4.5B fund too big https://twitter.com/...

“By making the language of Web3 meandering and impenetrable and by building a culture that is very self-referential, investors make criticism harder to come by.” https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...

“And so often when I read this stuff from [crypto] investors I feel like I'm being DDoS'd by marketing language, needless complexity, and vague future-casting.” https://newsletters.theatlantic.com/ ...
After freezing withdrawals, crypto exchange CoinFlex plans to issue up to 47M Recovery Value USD tokens, offering a 20% annual return, to repay a $47M debt
CoinFLEX's Tokenization Solution to Resume Withdrawals and Normal Operations
Crypto exchange CoinFlex is raising $47 million through a new coin after a major investor fails to pay debt
CoinFLEX to Launch a $47M Recovery Token to Solve Withdrawal Issues
CoinFLEX stops withdrawals due to “continued uncertainty involving a counterparty”
CoinFLEX to Issue New Token after Withdrawal Issues
CoinFLEX Halted Crypto Withdrawals. Now It Wants to Sell Its Bad Debt
CoinFLEX recovery plan includes tokenized bad debt and more yields
CoinFLEX plans new token to raise funds days after halting withdrawals
CoinFlex Issues New Token by Offering 20% Annual Return as a Solution to Re-enabling Withdrawals
CoinFlex Will Issue $47M Worth of “Recovery Tokens” After Withdrawal Freeze
CoinFLEX's USD 47M Recovery Token Plan Questioned by Crypto Observers
CoinFlex to resume withdrawals after raising funds via new token issuance

This is incredible. A centralized yield service, CoinFLEX, gave a $47m uncollateralized loan to someone who can't pay it right now. To fund other customers' withdrawals, they are now turning his debt into a token and selling it to people, offering 20% APY on it. Amazingly degen. https://twitter.com/...
Hi everyone, we're sharing our latest update on plans for re-enabling withdrawals on the platform 👇 https://coinflex.com/...

Recently CoinFlex paused withdrawals due to “extreme market conditions” This is, in my opinion, not an acceptable action for an exchange to take. According to their statement, “Withdrawals are estimated to begin June 30th” July 1st, I'll be posting my own statement. Thank you.

A lot of people suggesting that because CasCoin withdrawals have been halted for two weeks CasExchange is ruined. No. We are strategically raising $47 million from the CasRecoveryCoin, which will offer 21% APR paid in DoubleCasRecoveryToken. Confused? Good.

Hey everyone, I know you've been waiting to hear about the state of withdrawals on @CoinFLEXdotcom . We appreciate your patience. Here's an official update. 👇 https://www.bloomberg.com/... I went on @crypto @BloombergTV to talk about this today and it's time to go into more depth.
For people sad they missed out on risk-free 20% returns with Anchor, here's a new 20% yield crypto opportunity! https://twitter.com/...
Actually not a bad idea. Selling off a part of their book using a token. Run it. https://twitter.com/...
This is basically how I would imagine it would work if warhammer 40k orkz had financial institutions https://twitter.com/...
TLDR: send us all the money we lost, we promise we won't go bankrupt and will pay you back somehow, someday. Crypto continues to amaze me every day https://twitter.com/...

Crypto slowly “reinventing” all the ideas that have already blown up in tradFi, innovation at its finest. Only thing missing here is a crypto debt rating agency - someone call the VCs and devs, let's get a token going for a new debt rating agency for shitcoin CDOs, derivatives. https://twitter.com/...

We have temporarily paused withdrawals on @CoinFLEXdotcom as a protective measure. We are working to resume withdrawals as soon as possible. Please find our announcement here with more details. Thank you for your support. https://coinflex.com/...
TL;DR: someone supposedly wealthy and “high-integrity” missed margin calls and is in debt to CoinFLEX for $47M https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Another wild crypto story. CoinFlex has a big customer in debt. The individual can't repay + CF could not liquidate the account b/c of a special clause. So CoinFlex issued a new coin to raise $47M to plug the gap. Coin has 20% yield. What could go wrong? https://www.cnbc.com/...

Alright so @CoinFLEXdotcom is issuing rvUSD ("recovery value USD") to plug their balance sheet gap caused by an unnamed crypto whale. Only one whale I know of with those RV initials. Discuss. https://twitter.com/...

A verified insider has confirmed that the “high net worth individual” who owes CoinFLEX money is Bitcoin Cash advocate & CoinFLEX shareholder Roger Ver. Ver had a long on BCH, and the platform allowed him to run a deficit because he personally guaranteed he would pay them back. https://twitter.com/...

1. Take out $47M crypto loan 2. Refuse to pay it back 3. Wait for your lender to try to sell $47M in tokens to public promising a 20% yield to cover your debt 4. Buy tokens 5. Collect yield 6. Repay crypto loan 7. Profit and/or total market collapse https://twitter.com/...
I'm sure THIS time, the promise of a 20% risk-free annual return will absolutely come to pass. https://www.bloomberg.com/...
That's a Bitfinex move if I ever seen one. https://twitter.com/...

There were thousands upon thousands of bank failures in the 1920s / Great Depression due to runs on the bank. This resulted in the 1933 Banking Act, FDIC, and many stringent controls. 2020s - we're seeing runs on the bank and crazy solutions https://coinflex.com/...

They also pitch a pivot into a new model with increased transparency, pseudonymous balance tracking, and public liquidation points. Little do they know, they just accidentally reinvented DeFi. https://twitter.com/...
CoinFlex reportedly is planing to raise funds by issuing a new token. The token will have a 20% APR. They paused all withdraws 4 days ago. https://twitter.com/...

After Twitter gives Elon Musk access to its firehose and historical tweet data, experts doubt the data will provide conclusive answers on spam and bots

Even though I believe Elon Musk is complaining about bots to lower the acquisition price of Twitter, I have to admit the spam bot situation is bonkers. Every tweet I post has 2 spam replies within a minute. It's ridiculous how badly Twitter's doing here. https://www.wsj.com/...

Google Hangouts is being upgraded to #GoogleChat. Hangouts users can expect to be prompted to switch to Chat in Gmail or the Chat app. Read more to learn how Chat provides users a better experience by being more powerful, helpful and integrated → https://blog.google/... https://twitter.com/...
I put it to you that this problem would be solved once and for all if Google rationalized its messaging teams. Yes, I said “rationalized”, bite me. https://twitter.com/...

Google Hangouts is shutting down in November. Its successor is Google Chat, which is not the same as GChat, which was also known as Google Talk. None of those are the same as Spaces, which is the new name for Rooms. I am not making any of this up. https://www.theverge.com/...
It's pretty remarkable how bad Google and Facebook have been at creating new products over the last decade https://twitter.com/...
Google Hangouts is now Google Frenemy. https://twitter.com/...
ngl i miss the days of gchat inside gmail and how a new contact would pop up in chat if you'd been emailing them a whole bunch https://twitter.com/...

Google is now so deep in constantly renaming and shutting down chat apps that even the people building the products need a diagram to explain what the current names of their half dozen active chat apps are. https://twitter.com/...
Google has killed more chat tools than the number of texts any of them have seen https://twitter.com/...

As a customer, I am confused what got deprecated and what is still a thing. As a product manager, I am confused how product decisions are made to literally live out the messaging app meme for more than a decade. https://twitter.com/...

Never ceases to amaze me what Google is doing with their half a dozen chat products that are being deprecated parallel. Microsoft has Teams. Facebook has Workspace. Salesforce has Slack. Google has... I never know. https://twitter.com/...
hangouts chat allo duo meet voice wave what else this is hilarious https://twitter.com/...
My theory remains that Google keeps trying to schedule a big internal call to sort this out once and for all, but they can never agree on which app to use. https://twitter.com/...
Quarterly PSA of a name change. https://twitter.com/...
Not only is it impossible for me to keep track of all the different chat apps Google makes, but I also swore this was already happening? https://twitter.com/...

2032 - Google Chinwag will be sunsetted in favour of the new Google Natter protocol. But don't worry! Users of Google's Hot Air protocol can continue to use the service via Google Jaw-Jaw. Phew! https://twitter.com/...
Hangouts is now Chat. Chat is NOT Gchat. GChat is Talk. Talk is dead. Hope this helps https://twitter.com/...
How SCOTUS overturning Roe could be used to curb online freedoms and surveil vulnerable populations, as happened to sex workers following FOSTA/SESTA in 2018
My Body, My Data Act Tackles Online Privacy in Wake of Roe v. Wade Decision
Consumers swap period tracking apps in search of increased privacy following Roe v. Wade ruling
ADPPA US Privacy Law: Coming Soon in Wake of Roe v. Wade Redo
With Roe overturned, privacy advocates worry period tracking apps are sharing data, with partners or affiliates, that may become evidence of a crime
Stardust's privacy policy said it may share “anonymized, encrypted” data to third parties “whether or not legally required”, before being changed after pushback
If your only advice around cyber security and privacy in the reproductive justice space is to tell ppl to delete their period apps, politely, tap out of the conversation.

Only 2% of venture dollars in the U.S. go to women. It's thus sad but not unsurprising that the biggest capital allocators in venture capital consider the Supreme Court dialing back 50 years of progress in women's rights as a non-event. The surprise would be if they even noticed https://twitter.com/...
they have to comply with law enforcement requests. they are going to turn over the data. financial intermediaries too https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...
Update: In response to our reporting, Stardust changed its privacy policy to omit the phrase about cooperating with law enforcement “whether or not legally required.” https://www.vice.com/...

Read ⁦@MistressSnowPhD⁩ on sex workers as early warning smoke detectors of a growing carceral state. It's not surveillance capitalism so much as it is surveillance theocracy. https://www.wired.com/...

Of course most companies in their privacy policies say they comply with legal requests for data. But it's not binary: Google pushed back in court on the breadth of some location data warrants. But no acknowledgement of whether tech firms will try that here https://www.vice.com/... https://twitter.com/...

⚠️INSTAGRAM HAS TAKEN DOWN OUR PAGE.⚠️ Please help us continue to spread the word that https://abortionfinder.org/ is HERE to help folks access abortion care, and we aren't going ANYWHERE. https://twitter.com/...

Just one of so many ways surveillance capitalism and Big Tech infect our daily lives. (Some good resources here on the tech implications of overturning Roe v Wade: https://mayfirst.coop/...) https://www.vice.com/...

Developers of period trackers and fertility apps are working on ways to anonymize user data in response to the Supreme Court's ruling that struck down the constitutional right to an abortion. https://www.wsj.com/...

Apps that help people monitor their menstruation and fertility can also be troves of personal data; deleting the app isn't always enough to clean up your #digital breadcrumbs. #data #privacy https://www.wsj.com/... via @WSJ @CordiliaJames @sharatibken https://twitter.com/...

the number one period tracker on the app store just put it in writing that they'll voluntarily—without even being legally required to—hand over your info to law enforcement if asked https://www.vice.com/...

Technology that can be abused, will be abused, the only variable is time. Corporate policies change, laws change and leadership changes, if data is collected it's only a matter of time before it can be abused. https://twitter.com/...

“This removal of federal protections gives lawmakers a path to criminalizing any online discussion about abortion, restrictions that can easily extend to include reproductive health entirely.” https://twitter.com/...

“Sex workers are typically the first demographic to be targeted because we are excellent scapegoats; we're not only criminalized but also highly stigmatized, so the general public is less likely to hear us let alone believe us.” https://www.wired.com/...

“Sex workers, fully aware that the general public is unconcerned with our well-being, have already been forced to develop strategies and guides on how to evade detection...strategies that can help abortion seekers and more as the carceral state expands.” https://www.wired.com/...

“One of the most disturbing aspects of this financial discrimination raises the question: how do they know?” Excellent, alarming piece about the hungry surveillance apparatuses that are ready to track you in the post-Roe world https://twitter.com/...

The infrastructure for the surveillance and punishment of abortion-seekers already exists, and if you want to make sense of it, listen to sex workers who's been navigating and strategizing for survival. https://www.wired.com/...
A good (well, not good, the situation is dreadful, but you know what I mean), long read. https://twitter.com/...

“mercury is in retrograde, also i'm gonna give your period info to the cops” — stardust, basically read @samleecole on the #1 period tracker in the app store that will hand over your data without a warrant, and implications in a post-Roe US: https://www.vice.com/...
I wrote about abortion, FOSTA/SESTA, & why you should be listening to sex workers for @WIRED https://www.wired.com/...

Listen to the canaries in your communities. LGBTQI+ folks and sex workers been talking about the rise in christofascism for years, but so few ever heard us. Consider this, then, a canary's song. Are You Ready to Be Surveilled Like a Sex Worker?  | WIRED https://www.wired.com/...

Tech companies are silent about their users' data re abortion rights. That's a tacit acknowledgment that data is so valuable, that they would rather keep on collecting it, even if people are thrown in jail, fined or killed https://www.protocol.com/...

With Roe overturned, period-tracking apps raise new worries Period-tracking apps have been criticized for bad privacy practices. Here's how to track your cycle without sharing data with companies, employers, insurers or the government. https://www.washingtonpost.com/ ...

Re-upping this column I wrote in May, after the draft Dobbs opinion leaked. Tech companies still aren't saying what they will do when the inevitable data requests come from prosecutors... https://www.axios.com/...

.@Abortion_Finder is one of the easiest and most reliable ways to find information about your closest LEGAL abortion provider. It is a tool that uses data from @ANSIRH at UCSF to ensure folks have access to up-to-date information. Today @instagram removed their account. https://twitter.com/...

Abortion still is legal in many states. Why would @instagram take down the page!?!? This is censorship in an effort to control women and it needs to stop! If you or someone you know needs to find reproductive healthcare, visit: https://abortionfinder.org/ https://twitter.com/...

.@instagram giving into the “illegality” of abortion immediately rather than even attempting to take a stance is disappointing but not at all surprising. @facebook is very white, very tech bro male https://twitter.com/...

Corporations will hand your info over to law enforcement in a heartbeat. Corporations don't give one single fuck about your rights beyond what they can use for marketing. Your google searches. Your GPS directions. Your period trackers. They'll *automate* turning it over. https://twitter.com/...

Tech companies & data brokers collect & store SO much user data for long periods of time which makes them huge targets for subpoenas & law enforcement requests. Tech companies can try to come up with ways to stall or fight these requests but sometimes they literally can't say no.

Meta's new privacy policy goes into effect 7/26. Here's a screenshot of what data they collect. This is why women in states where abortion is banned SHOULD NOT communicate via Messenger or Instagram or post in groups. [Screenshots of Meta's updated privacy policy]
Analysis of Stardust period tracking app, the top free app on the US App Store on June 25, shows it shares users' phone numbers with analytics company Mixpanel
Tech Companies Won't Say If They'll Give Cops Abortion Data
Why US women are deleting their period tracking apps
TikTok Has a Few Main Ingredients for Making Money
Period tracker Stardust rolls back encryption claims amid scrutiny

Menstrual tracking app Stardust is one of Apple's top three most-downloaded free apps right now. It's also one of few apps that has said it will voluntarily—without being legally required to—comply with law enforcement if it's asked to share user data. https://www.vice.com/...

What we did was implement an encrypted wall between our users personally identifiable information (email/phone/apple id/ etc) and what they actually do on the Stardust app. When you create an account on Stardust you generate a unique encryption key (on your phone) (4/8)

New: we asked tech companies if they would provide data to police about users + abortions Facebook, Twitter, Snapchat, TikTok, Google, Amazon, Discord, Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile, Binance, Kraken, CashApp, Coinbase, Venmo, Uber and Lyft. None answered https://www.vice.com/...

Stardust said it would roll out end-to-end encryption so it would “not be able to hand over any of your period tracking data” to the government. After we reached out, Stardust quietly removed any mention of end-to-end encryption from its privacy policy. https://techcrunch.com/...

a few people recommended stardust when i put out a call for trustworthy period trackers, and on tiktok the company made a show of promising to protect its users :/ https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

TechCrunch asked the founders for more information about how the app is implementing end-to-end encryption. Stardust founder Moranis told TechCrunch that “all traffic to our servers is through standard SSL (hosted on AWS) and subsequent data storage on AWS RDS” jfc https://twitter.com/...

New: Period tracker Stardust rocketed to the top of Apple's app store over the weekend after Roe v. Wade was overturned. But the app shares the phone number of users (who give it when signing up) to a third-party analytics firm Mixpanel. w/ @sarahintampa https://techcrunch.com/...

Netflix plans to grow its investment in the Asia Pacific, which has 15% of Netflix's subscribers and is forecast to be the biggest driver of further expansion

A look at DALL-E Mini, a viral image-generation service by Hugging Face based on OpenAI's DALL-E offering good, absurd fun while showing how AI can warp reality
In recent weeks, DALL-E Mini has been serving up around 50,000 images a day. https://www.wired.com/...
The viral image generation app is good, absurd fun. It's also giving the world an education in how artificial intelligence may warp reality. https://www.wired.com/...

I spoke to the creators of the AI meme-machine Dall-E Mini. Beyond being great fun, they argue that it shows the value of opening up AI research. As image-generating AI systems improve, in future we may see a lot more controversial and misleading imagery. https://www.wired.com/...

Wow, that's a lot of shade thrown at VCs in one article @taavet ! Can't help but feel we should instead work together to grow the Europe tech ecosystem especially at times like this. Anyway, congrats on the fundraise!! - https://techcrunch.com/...

1/ When running @Bigpoint for any kind of operational guidance or perspective I had to turn to friends entrepreneurs as none of my investors had any kind of founder experience. It was such a game changer when @KeesKoolen & Rob Denton joined the board

6/ Very few attempts to build a new and scalable product in venture have succeeded but we think it's worth trying because if it works we could have GDP level impact on Europe and leave the European start-up ecosystem healthier than when we joined it https://techcrunch.com/...

1/ When I was building @Songkick, the most valuable investors I had were people who had built things themselves - Greg McAdoo, Paul Graham and Saul Klein had all been founders and CEOs. They helped me learn faster and were far more committed to helping us succeed

5/ This is why last year @seikatsu, @soundboy, @taavet & I have decided to build the product we wish we had when building. We're launching today @pluralplatform a €250m fund & investors with the scar tissue & reach to tilt the odds of success & quantum of impact for founders

1/When we started @TeleportInc with @keskkyla & @balajis back in 2014, we were at Sand Hill Road. Like physically. Not a metaphor. Eventually the team shifted towards Europe, but that round would not have happened here from scratch.
Interesting move, this @pluralplatform endeavour from @taavet, @soundboy, @KHelioui and @seikatsu Also interesting to see the Euro VC reactions to it :) https://tech.eu/...
‘Europe lacks founders turned investors. In Silicon Valley more than half the investors at top venture firms have built a company themselves. In Europe this figure is a pathetic 8%.’

Privacy advocates say the SmartLINK app, an alternative to ankle monitors, helped ICE increase the number of people it tracks from <6K to 230K+ in three years

What's it like to live with an app that the govt. requires you install on your phone? In collaboration with @themarkup, we @Documentedny/@Report4America dug into SmartLINK, an app that ICE uses to track immigrants as they await court dates. W/@suryamattu https://documentedny.com/...

Digital surveillance is touted as a humane alternative to detention, but immigrants say it has created a virtual prison that has profoundly affected their lives. With @themarkup, we investigated ICE's SmartLINK app that it uses to track 230K immigrants. https://documentedny.com/...

Reading another great story from @themarkup about how ICE is using a mobile app to surveil immigrants. And by “great” I mean thorough and well reported. But it's not a feel good story. It's yet another example of tech weaponized to harm the vulnerable. https://themarkup.org/...

ICE has drastically expanded a program that requires immigrants awaiting court decisions to conduct regular check-ins via a mobile monitoring app. 230,000 people currently use it. What we know about the technology—and its privacy risks—with @documentedny: https://themarkup.org/...

I've seen asylum seekers be given phones from ICE that are only for this check in app, but this article sounds like some folks are having the app installed on their personal phones? Anyone have clarification on that? https://documentedny.com/...

“They took off my ankle monitor and they're making me use that app. It's as if I still had it [the ankle monitor] on.” With @themarkup, we spoke to immigrants about the stress caused by an app ICE uses to monitor their movements in the U.S. https://documentedny.com/...
